title: AST Deceased male: Fairbanks, AK [AK25083370]
summary: On August 18, 2025, the Alaska State Troopers responded to the area of Chena Ridge for a death investigation. Troopers investigated and determined the death was a suicide. The body has been taken to the medical examiner's office. Next of Kin has been notified.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

summary: U.S. President Donald Trump meets with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy at the White House on Aug. 18, 2025 in Washington, D.C. title: Bureau of Indian Affairs Reduces Anchorage Enrollment Services Hours
summary: The Bureau of Indian Affairs (BIA) has announced that its Anchorage Enrollment Services office will have new, reduced operating hours beginning September 3, 2025. It will be open to the public on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. until further notice. Earlier this year, the BIA...

title: Mary Peltola would be the strongest likely candidate for Alaska governor, poll shows
summary: This story was originally published by The 19th , an independent, nonprofit newsroom reporting on gender, politics and policy. Grace Panetta The 19th Former Democratic Rep. Mary Peltola holds a commanding lead in the 2026 election for governor of Alaska if she chooses to jump into the race, a new survey from progressive pollster Data for Progress shows. Peltola, Yup’ik, who was elected to Alaska’s at-large U.S. House seat in a 2022 special election and then to a full term in the House before losing her seat in 2024, is widely viewed as the most formidable potential Democratic candidate for either governor or U.S. Senate in 2026. She was the first woman to represent Alaska in the U.S. House, the first Alaska Native person elected to Congress and could be the state’s first Alaska Native gov...

title: Monday, August 18, 2025
summary: Photo: Knight Hall at the University of Wyoming in Laramie, Wyo. (Kamila Kudelska / Wyoming Public Media) Eastern Shoshone and Northern Arapaho students will have a crack at a new scholarship at the University of Wyoming (UW). Wyoming Public Radio’s Hannah Habermann has more. UW is creating the “Wind River Promise Fund”. Two hundred and fifty thousand dollars will be available to tribal members to cover undergrad tuition costs and...

title: Trump wants states to feed voter info into powerful citizenship data program
summary: People participate in a naturalization ceremony last year at Liberty State Park in Jersey City, N.J. The Trump administration is encouraging states to use an online search tool to verify the citizenship of registered voters, alarming some Democrats and privacy experts. (Photo by Michael M. Santiago/Getty Images) BILOXI, Miss. — The Trump administration is developing a powerful data tool it claims will let states identify noncitizens registered to vote. But Democratic critics and data experts warn it could allow the federal government to vacuum up vast quantities of information on Americans for unclear purposes. Some Democratic election officials and opponents of the effort fear President Donald Trump wants to build a federal database of voters to target political opponents or cherry-pick rare examples of noncitizen voters to fuel a sense of crisis. Republican election officials allied with the president counter that he’s helping states to maintain accurate voter rolls. The Trump admini...

title: AST Search and Rescue: Anchorage [AK25082937]
summary: On August 17, 2025, at 6:46pm, the Alaska State Troopers received a report of an injured hiker on Little O'Malley Peak, near Anchorage. title: AST Death Investigation: Lower Kalskag [AK25082768]
summary: On August 17, 2025, at 7:45am, the Alaska State Troopers received a report of the death of Sophia Mallette, age 29 of Lower Kalskag. Investigation revealed her death was not suspicious. Mallette’s body is being transported to the State Medical Examiner’s Office. Next of kin have been notified.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Traffic: Fairbanks [AK25082737]
summary: On 08/17/2025 at approximately 02:07 a traffic stop was initiated on a black truck traveling on Davis Rd. The driver, Valerie Jean Circle age 44 of Fairbanks, was determined to be intoxicated, and was placed under arrest for DUI. She was remanded to FCC with no further incident.
